name: Grilled Chicken Breasts with Cilantro-Lime Butter
recipeIngredient: 2 Split Chicken Breasts; with wings, skin and bones (1 1/2 pounds)
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: For the cilantro-lime butter, cream the butter. Grate 1/2 teaspoon zest from the lime and squeeze 1 1/2 teaspoons juice into the butter. Beat in the shallot, cilantro, salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper. Roll mixture into a 1-inch log of plastic wrap and set aside. Refrigerate or freeze up to 1 month.
For chicken and marinade: Remove the top two thirds of each chicken wing, leaving the segment nearest the breast meat attached. Trim off the knobby wing joints and scrape around the bones. Sprinkle with 1 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon pepper. Squeeze 2 tablespoons lime juice into a nonreactive baking dish. Stir in oil and cilantro. Turn to coat each side of chicken breasts in the marinade. Set chicken aside at room temperature for 2 hours (Can cover and refrigerate overnight).
Cooking: If the cilantro-lime butter has been refrigerated, bring it to room temperature. Heat a grill or adjust oven rack to high position. Grill or broil the chicken breasts until juices run clear when pierced in the thickest part of the breast, turning once, 7 to 8 minutes per side.
Serving: Transfer chicken breasts to warm dinner plates and top each with a tablespoon of herb butter.
